There is a big comet named Vasaketu which has an elongated
glossy body stretched towards the north. It rises in the west. Its
appearance coincides with dreadful mortality, but it leads to very
good growth of food crops in the land.
II^oh
There is one named Asthiketu, endowed with all the charac-
teristics of Vasaketu, but is rough and capable of causing famine.
There is another Ketu named Sastra, which is similar to the
previous one, but glossy and rising in the east. It leads to war and
pestilence.
[Para am gives some more details about these comets :

There is a comet named Kapalaketu, which is visible during
the Am ivasya—new moon—in the east. Its rays and crest are of
smoky colour and it traverses half the heavens. It produces famine,
pestilence, drought and diseases.
